3. Hacking into the computer system of the University of Southern California (USC), where he stole passwords and other sensitive information.

However, in 1995, Mitnick was arrested and charged with various crimes related to his hacking activities. He was sentenced to 46 months in prison and ordered to pay $2.2 million in restitution.

After his release from prison in 2000, Mitnick turned his life around and became a cybersecurity consultant and speaker.
